title: Confirmation Receipt Gap Hunting
description: A reconciliation and back-office workflow that resolves breaks, validates accounting alignment, and closes audit-critical exceptions. Focus case: confirmation receipt gap hunting.
assumptions: Each reconciliation case has a unique identifier and stable candidate match relationships are determinable by referenced matching predicates.
Ledger entries, trade legs, and external confirmations include sufficient metadata (unique keys, amounts, dates) to evaluate matches deterministically.
Operators and automated agents have the necessary permissions to attach evidence and consume approval tokens.
External feeds and attachment stores are available and their records are immutable once linked to a case for audit traceability.
risks: False positive matches due to incomplete or ambiguous identifiers that lead to incorrect case closure.
Stale or delayed external confirmations that cause repeated escalation and potential duplicate work.
Improper consumption or reuse of approval tokens or ledger entries causing auditability gaps.
Insufficient evidence capture (missing attachments or metadata) preventing defensible audit closure.
